description_long: This object is a vintage-style slide projector, primarily constructed from metal and plastic, with a boxy main body and a cylindrical lens assembly protruding from the front. The main housing is a light gray color, featuring a visible ventilation grill on the top for heat dissipation. On one side, a label reading 'ORLUX N24' is affixed, suggesting a specific model or brand. The front section includes a large lens with adjustment rings, and a slide tray mechanism is visible, designed to hold and insert photographic slides for projection. The base is equipped with small feet for stability, and the overall design is utilitarian, with a focus on function over aesthetics. The object appears robust and suitable for use in educational, office, or public presentation settings.
description: A robust, vintage-style slide projector with a boxy gray body, cylindrical lens, and slide tray mechanism, featuring a visible ventilation grill and model label.
description_view_0: Side view showing the 'ORLUX N24' label, the boxy main body, and the cylindrical lens assembly.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the large lens, adjustment rings, and the extended slide tray with a photographic slide inserted.
description_view_2: Angled view displaying the slide tray mechanism, lens assembly, and the top ventilation grill.
description_view_3: Rear-side view showing the back of the main housing, the slide tray partially removed, and the overall rectangular shape.
attribute_brand_logo: The label 'ORLUX N24' is visible on the lower side of the main body, in white text on a gray background, indicating the model and possibly the brand.
attribute_lens_type: Fixed lens with a large diameter, featuring adjustment rings for focus; likely a standard or short-throw projection lens.
attribute_control_panel_design: No detailed control panel is visible; possible single power button and focus dial located near the lens assembly.
attribute_ventilation_pattern: A rectangular ventilation grill with a mesh or slatted pattern is located on the top surface of the main housing.
